records.summary: This is a typed PTCA (Percutaneous Transluminal Coronary Angioplasty) report detailing a PCI procedure with two drug-eluting stents deployed in the OM1 artery. The report includes catheter and guide wire details, pre- and post-dilatation parameters, stent specifications, and a TIMI flow grade of 3. It also notes the risk of stent thrombosis and re-stenosis.
key_observations: Procedure: PCI with two XIENCE PRIME stents deployed in OM1
Stent sizes: 2.5*23 mm and 2.5*28 mm
TIMI flow grade: 3 (good end result)
Procedure date: 03/04/2026
Stent expiration dates: 2027-03-12 and 2026-12-05
Patient age/sex: 63 Y / M

ocr.verbatim: PATIENT NAME: REF BY: MD DNB CARDIO DATE: 04/04/2026 X-RAY CHEST A/P VIEW â€˘ No pneumothorax. â€˘ Both lung fields show prominent bronchovascular markings. â€˘ No collapse, consolidation cavity or mass lesion seen. â€˘ No active Koch's lesion seen. â€˘ Cardiac shadow normal. â€˘ Both hila appear normal. â€˘ Both domes normal. â€˘ Both costophrenic angles clear. â€˘ Bony ribs under vision no displaced fracture SUGGEST: clinical correlation. Thanks for reference. M.D. RE -17187 M.D. Consultant Radiologist & Sonologist Ex. Asst. Professor, Civil Hosp. A'bad Subject to Polanpur Jurisdiction Only | Not Valid for any Medico Legal Purpose

The report lists findings including no pneumothorax, prominent bronchovascular markings, no collapse or mass lesions, normal cardiac shadow, and no displaced fractures. It suggests clinical correlation and includes the radiologist's signature and credentials.
key_observations: Report is for an X-ray chest A/P view dated 04/04/2026.
Findings include no pneumothorax, prominent bronchovascular markings, and no active Koch's lesion.
Normal cardiac shadow, hila, domes, and costophrenic angles reported.
Report suggests clinical correlation and includes radiologist's signature and credentials.
Document contains redaction and disclaimer text.
